title: D-CHIRO-INOSITOL
description: D-chiro-inositol (DCI) is a naturally occurring stereoisomer of inositol that functions as a secondary messenger in insulin signaling pathways. It works by improving insulin sensitivity and glucose metabolism, primarily through replenishment of insulin-mediating phosphoglycans. DCI is most commonly used to manage polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), where it helps restore ovulatory function, reduce androgen levels, and improve metabolic parameters.

research.title: Ovulatory and Metabolic Effects of d-Chiro-Inositol in the Polycystic Ovary Syndrome
research.summary: In 44 obese women with PCOS, 1200mg daily of DCI significantly improved insulin sensitivity, reduced free testosterone by 54%, lowered blood pressure and triglycerides, and restored ovulation in 86% of women compared to 27% in placebo group.

research.title: Comparative efficacy of combined myo-inositol and D-chiro inositol versus metformin across PCOS Phenotypes
research.summary: A 12-week trial showed that MI+DCI combination (40:1 ratio) significantly improved insulin sensitivity, SHBG levels, ovarian volume, menstrual regularity, BMI, quality of life, and stress scores in PCOS patients, with comparable efficacy to metformin.

research.title: D-Chiro-inositol and its significance in polycystic ovary syndrome: a systematic review
research.summary: This systematic review consolidates evidence showing DCI's role in improving insulin resistance, hormonal profiles, and ovulatory function in PCOS patients through its action as an insulin mediator.

research.title: Efficacy of myo-inositol and d-chiro-inositol combination on menstrual cycle regulation and improving insulin resistance in young women with polycystic ovary syndrome
research.summary: Randomized controlled trial demonstrated that MI+DCI combination effectively regulated menstrual cycles and improved insulin resistance in young women with PCOS.

sentiment.summary: Users with PCOS report significant improvements in menstrual regularity, ovulation, insulin sensitivity, and hormonal balance. Most users recommend it as a first-line treatment for PCOS symptoms, particularly when combined with myo-inositol at the 40:1 ratio.

effects.label: PCOS Management:
effects.description: Highly effective for managing PCOS symptoms, with comparable or superior results to metformin in some studies, particularly for phenotypes C and D

effects.label: Insulin Sensitivity:
effects.description: Produces dramatic improvements in insulin action, with plasma insulin curves decreasing by over 60% in clinical trials

effects.label: Phenotype-Specific Response:
effects.description: May be particularly effective for certain PCOS phenotypes, especially those without severe metabolic dysfunction

effects.label: Combination Therapy:
effects.description: Works best when combined with myo-inositol at 40:1 ratio (MI:DCI), providing synergistic benefits for both metabolic and reproductive outcomes

dosage.label: Standard Dose:
dosage.description: Most clinical trials used 1200mg daily of DCI alone, showing significant therapeutic effects at this dosage

dosage.label: Combination Ratio:
dosage.description: When combined with myo-inositol, optimal ratio is 40:1 (MI:DCI), which mirrors the physiological ratio in human tissues

dosage.label: Duration:
dosage.description: Treatment periods of 6-12 weeks show measurable improvements, with continued benefits observed with ongoing supplementation

dosage.label: Split Dosing:
dosage.description: Some users report better tolerance and efficacy when splitting daily dose into two administrations

sideEffects.label: Generally Well-Tolerated:
sideEffects.description: Clinical trials report minimal adverse effects, with most patients completing full treatment courses without issues

sideEffects.label: Gastrointestinal Symptoms:
sideEffects.description: Some users may experience mild digestive upset, particularly when initiating therapy or at higher doses

sideEffects.label: Ovarian Response:
sideEffects.description: In rare cases, excessive DCI supplementation may negatively impact oocyte quality; maintaining proper MI:DCI ratio is important

sideEffects.label: Hypoglycemia Risk:
sideEffects.description: Improved insulin sensitivity may require adjustment of diabetic medications to prevent low blood sugar in susceptible individuals

availability.label: Widely Available:
availability.description: DCI is readily available as a dietary supplement both standalone and in combination formulas with myo-inositol

availability.label: Quality Considerations:
availability.description: 40:1 MI:DCI ratio products are specifically formulated for PCOS and are preferred over standalone DCI for most users

availability.label: Prescription Status:
availability.description: Available over-the-counter as a nutritional supplement in most countries, though some practitioners may recommend specific pharmaceutical-grade formulations
